reasoning: The TGA balance as of late October 2025 was unusually high at $905B, likely reflecting a post-debt-limit 'rebuild' phase. Treasury typically targets a lower end-of-quarter cash balance limit (historically $750B - $850B). Because the balance is currently elevated, it is more likely to drift downward toward its target over the coming months as Treasury balances issuance against ongoing deficit spending. November is typically a net deficit month with no major tax deadlines before December 15, making it probable that the TGA will fall from early November to early December. For the immediate 1-week horizon (Nov 16), normal start-to-mid month spending without major corresponding tax receipts often drains the account slightly before mid-month auction settlements on the 15th (which fall after the likely Wed Nov 12 observation date for Nov 16). For the 3-month horizon (Feb 2026), uncertainty is significantly higher given intervening mid-December and mid-January tax receipts, though the structural downward drift back to baseline targets favors a slightly below-50% probability of increase. In accordance with principles, uncertainty increases (probabilities move closer to 0.5) for the more distant resolution dates.
batch_queries: Will the amount of money held by the US Treasury in its general account at the Federal Reserve Bank of New York have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-11-09?
